stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In hydrology, a watershed is an area of land that drains all its surface water into a single outlet.
  • Watersheds are commonly named with a two-part designation.
  • The first part identifies the receiving body of water, while the second part identifies a defining geographic feature.
  • The Mississippi Delta watershed, for example, drains into the Mississippi River near its alluvial outlet.
  • The Columbia Plateau watershed drains into the Columbia River across a basalt plateau region.
  • This naming convention is based on the work of an American hydrographer named John Wesley Powell (1834–1902).
stem: The student wants to provide an example of a watershed. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Watersheds are areas of land that drain all their surface water into a single outlet.
The watershed naming convention uses two-part designations and is based on the work of John Wesley Powell, an American hydrographer.
One example of a watershed is the Columbia Plateau watershed, which drains into the Columbia River across a basalt plateau region.
Watersheds are commonly named with a two-part designation that indicates a receiving body of water and a geographic feature.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. It provides a specific example of a watershed, the Columbia Plateau watershed, satisfying the goal.

Choice A gives a general definition without an example. Choice B describes the naming convention rather than naming an example. Choice D explains how watersheds are classified rather than offering a specific example.
